claim_chain: Lamina I projection neurons densely innervated by Trpm8 afferents correspond to cold-selective cells
These neurons receive monosynaptic input from Trpm8 afferents
Calbindin can be used as a molecular marker to target these cells
Cold-selective neurons project to specific brain regions involved in thermosensation
Direct monosynaptic input from Trpm8 afferents is substantial
Findings provide insights into circuits underlying thermoregulation and cold perception

typical_pattern: Provide supporting evidence from current study

likely_priorities: Specificity of molecular markers for cell populations
Quantitative rigor in estimating synaptic proportions
likely_attacks: Calbindin is not a specific marker for cold-selective neurons
Discrepancy with Lee et al. findings on Trhr-expressing interneurons
Incomplete labeling of Trpm8 afferents may affect quantitative estimates
Lack of paradoxical heat response may reflect methodological limitations
Caution needed in relying on single genetic markers
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'at present, there is no known molecular marker that could be used to target cold-selective lamina I ALS cells exclusively' and discusses why calbindin was chosen despite limitations. It extensively addresses the Lee et al. study findings that conflict with their observations, noting 'this observation is not consistent with our transcriptomic dataset' and 'it is not clear why a brain projection from Trhr cells was not detected.' The authors acknowledge that 'this line only labels ~83% of Trpm8-expressing primary afferent neurons with GFP' affecting quantitative estimates. They discuss the absence of paradoxical heat responses and suggest methodological reasons. The paper concludes with 'the need for caution in relying on the specificity of individual genetic markers.'

examples.quoted_phrase: we directly demonstrate the presence of synapses with both electron microscopy and optogenetics
this suggests that these sites are directly innervated by cold-selective ALS neurons in lamina I
our findings clearly demonstrate a major direct (monosynaptic) input from Trpm8-expressing primary afferents to cold-selective lamina I projection neurons
it is likely that both populations are included amongst the GFP-expressing cells
indicate the need for caution in relying on the specificity of individual genetic markers

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based entirely on the provided discussion, abstract, and figure legend content.
